title: Declarative Validation
text: Declarative Validation In declarative validation, Uniface checks the data against constraints in entity and field definitions. Uniface always checks declarative constraints before procedural constraints. Most declarative constraints are at field level and are defined by the Data Type, Field Syntax, and ValRep properties. For more information, see Field-Level Declarative Constraints . At entity level, declarative validation constraints are defined by specifying a minimum or maximum number of occurrences for the entity, or defining a compound key. (New or modified keys must be complete.) For more information, see Entity-Level Declarative Constraints . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
